options.text: No — solving gives $x = 29.5$, which is not a whole number of tables.

options.why: Rounded $29.5$ down to force a whole-number answer instead of rejecting it.
Rounded up; $30$ tables give $2(30)+4=64$ chairs, not $63$.
Solved for $x$ but ignored that the number of tables must be a whole number.

questions.solution_text:
$2x + 4 = 63$
$2x = 59$
$x = 29.5$
Since $29.5$ is not a whole number, exactly $63$ chairs is not possible.

options.why: Added the fixed $2$ instead of subtracting it: $(202+2)\div4$.
Divided by $2$ instead of the coefficient $4$ after subtracting: $200\div2$.
Subtracted the fixed $2$ but forgot to divide by $4$.
